text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: We all know that some people do not seem as emotionally strong as others
zh: [1] 我们都知道，在生活遭遇困境时，有些人似乎不像其他人那样情绪坚强。

text: when life gets difficult. But why is that?
zh: [2] 但这是为什么呢？

text: A study published in 2003 in the journal Science offered an answer.
zh: [3] 2003年发表在《科学》杂志上的一项研究给出了答案。

text: The study followed almost 850 people from birth through age 26.
zh: [4] 该研究追踪了近850人，从出生一直到26岁。

text: Researchers found that those with a short version of a certain gene
zh: [5] 研究人员发现，携带某种基因短版本的人

text: were more likely to get depressed after a sad or difficult experience.
zh: [6] 在经历悲伤或困难的事件后更容易患上抑郁症。

text: They found that people with the normal length of the gene were better able to deal with problems.
zh: [7] 他们还发现，携带正常长度基因的人更能应对问题。

text: The gene is a transporter of serotonin,
zh: [8] 这种基因是血清素的转运体，

text: a brain chemical involved with mood and desire for food.
zh: [9] 血清素是一种与情绪和食欲有关的大脑化学物质。

text: The 2003 study captured attention among mental health professionals and popular culture.
zh: [10] 2003年的这项研究在心理健康专业人士和大众文化中都引起了关注。

text: In fact, Science magazine recognized the discovery
zh: [11] 事实上，《科学》杂志

text: of genes for mental illness as the number two breakthrough of the year.
zh: [12] 将这项关于精神疾病基因的发现评为当年第二大突破。

text: The winner was observations about mysteries of the universe.
zh: [13] 第一名是关于宇宙奥秘的观测发现。

text: In June, Soon however, other researchers published findings of a large new study.
zh: [14] 然而不久后，也就是6月，其他研究人员发表了一项大型新研究的结果。

text: They reported finding no link between the serotonin transporter gene and the risk of depression.
zh: [15] 他们报告称，未发现血清素转运基因与抑郁风险之间存在关联。

text: The findings appeared in the Journal of the American Medical Association.
zh: [16] 这项研究结果发表在《美国医学会杂志》上。

text: Neil Reich is director of the University of California
zh: [17] 尼尔·赖希是加州大学

text: San Francisco Institute for Human Genetics and a leader of the new study.
zh: [18] 旧金山分校人类遗传学研究所的所长，也是这项新研究的负责人。

text: He says the earlier study gained so much recognition
zh: [19] 他表示，早期那项研究获得了如此多的关注，

text: it became fixed in many people's minds as true.
zh: [20] 以至于在许多人心中已被当作事实固定下来。

text: The National Institute of Mental Health and Kaiser Permanente Northern California
zh: [21] 美国国家心理健康研究所和北加州凯撒医疗机构

text: also took part in the latest study.
zh: [22] 也参与了这项最新研究。

text: The researchers used information from 14 studies involving more than 14 ,000 patients.
zh: [23] 研究人员使用了来自14项研究、涉及超过14000名患者的数据。

text: The scientists examined the data
zh: [24] 科学家们对这些数据进行了分析，

text: using the same measures as the 2003 study.
zh: [25] 使用了与2003年研究相同的测量方法。

text: They found that the risk of depression was not higher among those with the shorter gene.
zh: [26] 他们发现，携带较短基因的人群患抑郁症的风险并没有更高。

text: But they also found that stressful events themselves
zh: [27] 但他们也发现，压力事件本身

text: did appear to increase the risk for depression.
zh: [28] 确实似乎会增加患抑郁症的风险。

text: Neuroscientist Avshalom Caspi, then at King's College London,
zh: [29] 神经科学家阿夫沙洛姆·卡斯皮当时在伦敦国王学院任职，

text: led the 2003 study. He is now at Duke University in North Carolina.
zh: [30] 主导了2003年的那项研究。他现在在北卡罗来纳州的杜克大学任教。

text: He has criticized the new study as incomplete.
zh: [31] 他批评这项新研究并不完整。

text: He says it ignores evidence that that supports the original research.
zh: [32] 他说，这项研究忽略了支持原始研究的证据。

text: Peter Zandi is a genetic researcher at Johns Hopkins University School of Public Health in Maryland.
zh: [33] 彼得·赞迪是马里兰州约翰霍普金斯大学公共卫生学院的遗传学研究员。

text: He agrees that this latest study is not the final word.
zh: [34] 他也认同这项最新研究并非定论。

text: He said, after many years of trying
zh: [35] 他说，在多年努力

text: to figure out what is going on with the genetic cause of depression, we're still not there yet.
zh: [36] 试图弄清抑郁症遗传成因的过程中，我们仍未得出最终答案。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [37] 以上就是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: For more health news, go to VOASpecialEnglish .com.
zh: [38] 欲了解更多健康新闻，请访问VOASpecialEnglish.com。
